description: FGIP thesis receipt — tracks source-backed funding-chain evidence for investment theses. The graph remains off-chain; only hashes, metadata, and lifecycle state go on-chain.
threshold: Promotion requires: (1) 3+ independent signal source types, (2) at least 1 Tier 0 government source, (3) counter-thesis articulated and assessed, (4) conviction score >= 60

state_mapping.Candidate: Thesis proposed, evidence collection in progress
state_mapping.Active: Triangulation met, Tier 0 confirmed, counter-thesis assessed, conviction >= 3
state_mapping.Quarantined: Disconfirming evidence found or counter-thesis validated

a_plus_b_equals_c.A: Congress authorizes defense funding (NDAA $895.2B, Ukraine supplemental $60.84B)
a_plus_b_equals_c.B: DoD / USASpending / company records connect funding to primes and suppliers
a_plus_b_equals_c.C: Defense-prime funding chain is active with multi-year replenishment cycle

funding_chain: Prime contractors receive awards (LMT, RTX, NOC, GD, HII)
Subcontractors supply components (BWXT naval reactors, HWM forgings)

evidence_tiers.tier_0: Government records: Congress.gov bill text, USASpending contracts, EDGAR 13F, Federal Register rulemakings
evidence_tiers.tier_1: Professional sources: earnings calls, company press releases, Reuters/WSJ

notes: All thesis evidence (source articles, extracted facts, proposed edges, full receipt JSON) stays off-chain in the FGIP database. On-chain stores only: content_hash (receipt identity), original_hash (evidence bundle identity), metadata_hash (thesis metadata identity), receipt hashes (validation proofs), status (Candidate/Active/Quarantined), and timestamps. No private data, no personal capital info, no financial advice.
